seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with BeeAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with BeeAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building a production RAG pipeline with BeeAI enables robust document retrieval, context assembly, and LLM synthesis with source attribution for reliable AI agents. However, common failure modes like unbounded agent memory, tool injection vulnerabilities, serialization errors, and model output inconsistencies can compromise reliability and security. Vex provides essential guardrails through event listening to verify source faithfulness, detect hallucinations, ensure context relevance, and validate citations in high-risk deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with BeeAI
architectureOverview.description:
The BeeAI RAG pipeline follows a standard retrieval-augmented generation flow: user queries trigger vector search across document embeddings, retrieving top-k relevant chunks. BeeAI's agent memory maintains conversation context while tools handle retrieval and citation assembly. Retrieved context is injected into the Granite LLM prompt for synthesis, with BeeAI's event system enabling observability at each stage.

BeeAI agents orchestrate this via custom tools for vector search (using FAISS or Pinecone) and context formatting, persisting state across sessions. The framework's memory management and tool validation are critical, but require Vex monitoring to catch drift in long conversations or injection attempts. Final LLM output includes source attribution metadata for traceability.

Vex integrates as an event listener on BeeAI's agent events, capturing retrieval results, context assembly, and final synthesis for verification against hallucinations and citation accuracy.

risks.name: Unbounded Agent Memory Growth
risks.description: Long RAG conversations accumulate retrieval contexts without eviction, causing OOM errors and degraded relevance
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors memory events and enforces context window limits with relevance-based eviction

risks.name: Tool Injection via Malicious Queries
risks.description: Adversarial inputs bypass BeeAI tool validation, injecting false documents or bypassing retrieval
risks.mitigation: Vex validates tool inputs/outputs against schema and detects injection patterns

risks.name: Hallucination Despite Retrieved Context
risks.description: Granite model generates unsupported claims even with relevant context provided
risks.mitigation: Vex performs source faithfulness verification on final output against retrieved chunks

risks.name: Citation Drift in Multi-Turn Sessions
risks.description: Source attribution becomes inaccurate as conversation context grows and memory serializes
risks.mitigation: Vex tracks citation lineage across agent state persistence events

implementation.title: BeeAI RAG Agent with Vector Retrieval Tool
implementation.description: Core agent setup with FAISS vector store tool for document retrieval
implementation.code:
from beeai import Agent, Tool
from beeai.memory import ConversationBufferMemory
import faiss
import numpy as np

# Initialize vector store (pre-built embeddings)
doc_embeddings = np.random.rand(1000, 384).astype('float32')
index = faiss.IndexFlatL2(384)
index.add(doc_embeddings)

doc_chunks = ["chunk1 text...", "chunk2 text..."]  # Your documents

@Tool()
def retrieve_docs(query: str, top_k: int = 5) -> list:
    '''Retrieve relevant document chunks'''
    query_emb = np.random.rand(1, 384).astype('float32')  # Your embedding model
    distances, indices = index.search(query_emb, top_k)
    return [{"content": doc_chunks[i], "score": float(distances[0][j])} 
            for j, i in enumerate(indices[0])]

rag_agent = Agent(
    tools=[retrieve_docs],
    memory=ConversationBufferMemory(),
    model="ibm/granite-13b-chat"
)

implementation.title: Context Assembly and Synthesis Prompt
implementation.description: Format retrieved context for Granite LLM with citation tracking

implementation.title: Source Attribution in Agent Response
implementation.description: Extract and validate citations from Granite output

implementation.title: Multi-Turn RAG Conversation Handler
implementation.description: Handle conversation state with memory limits
implementation.code:
class RAGConversation:
    def __init__(self, agent):
        self.agent = agent
        self.max_memory_tokens = 8000
    
    async def chat(self, query: str):
        # Retrieve fresh context
        docs = await self.agent.tools[0].run(query)
        
        # Check memory limits
        if self.agent.memory.token_count > self.max_memory_tokens:
            self.agent.memory.prune_oldest()
        
        response = await self.agent.run(query, context=docs)
        
        # Validate output
        validation = validate_citations(response, docs)
        return {
            'response': response,
            'citations': validation,
            'sources': docs
        }
